## Description
Provide a clear description of the changes introduced by this Pull Request. Include relevant context, rationale, and motivation for the changes.

## Related Issues
Closes # (or fixes #)

## Type of Change
Tick the option(s) that apply (or check them directly in the GitHub UI after opening the PR).
- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Code style / Refactoring (formatting, local variables)
- [ ] DevOps / CI/CD pipeline adjustment

## How Has This Been Tested?
Please describe the tests that you ran to verify your changes. Provide instructions so we can reproduce.

- [ ] **Backend Tests:** Run `pnpm --dir backend test` (or describe manual API verification)
- [ ] **Frontend Tests:** Run `pnpm test` in the frontend directory

## Checklist
- [ ] My code follows the style guidelines of this project (PEP 8 for Python, ESLint/Prettier for React).
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code.
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas.
- [ ] I have updated the documentation (`README.md`, API endpoints list, etc.) accordingly.
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings or console errors.
- [ ] The automated GitHub Action pipelines pass successfully.

## Screenshots (if applicable)
Add screenshots or GIFs demonstrating any frontend UI/UX updates.
